dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Hmm, it's not the first time the kids have reacted like this.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: But I have to watch them do this in the classroom.

dialogue.speaker: Chairman
dialogue.line: Congratulations to all new students starting at Ye Ran High School.

dialogue.speaker: Chairman
dialogue.line: Our school first and foremost values the free will as a virtue.

dialogue.speaker: Chairman
dialogue.line: Hmm, I hope you spend a wonderful time here for the next three years.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: What kind of school has students that are disappointed that the Chairman's speech is over?

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Mr. Park, please take care of him. He's been living abroad and unfamiliar with Korean culture. Please be generous with any misunderstandings that might arise, as he absolutely does not have any bad intentions.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: As you might have guessed, this is your new classmate.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: He was born and raised abroad, and it's his first time in Korea. He's rather clueless about the culture here, so I'm counting on you all to help him out.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: I don't know either; (Right, his name yet)

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Shit, again? It's like this morning with Shinwoo. I have never heard this language in my life; how should I know what he's saying?

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: I can't just brush it off like last time either.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Phew, what's with the long name?

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Sounds like a name from a royal family.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Hey, he's right; you can speak Korean!

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: You have a long name. What's the first name and what's the last name?

dialogue.speaker: Cadis Etrama Di Raizel
dialogue.line: All of it is the first name and last name.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Hmm... Hm? That's possible? What should we call you then?

dialogue.speaker: Cadis Etrama Di Raizel
dialogue.line: You may call me whatever is convenient for you, Sir.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: It was so natural that I almost missed it.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: He's not addressing you as 'Teacher' or 'Sir'.

summary: New students are welcomed to Ye Ran High School by the Chairman. A new transfer student, Cadis Etrama Di Raizel, is introduced, who initially speaks an unknown language and expresses a desire for noodles. He then reveals his incredibly long name and speaks Korean, but in a very formal and unusual manner, surprising his new classmates.
